# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent service mesh security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: install.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: IstioOperator
metadata:
  name: istio-mesh-config
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  meshConfig:
    # Allow outbound traffic to external services without requiring ServiceEntry definitions
    # This provides flexibility for microservices integrating with external APIs
    outboundTrafficPolicy:
      mode: ALLOW_ANY

    # Enforce mTLS across the mesh to secure all service-to-service communication
    # Even though outbound is permissive, internal traffic remains encrypted and authenticated
    defaultConfig:
      holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true
      proxyMetadata: {}
      # Enable access logging for audit and observability
      accessLogFile: /dev/stdout
      accessLogEncoding: JSON

    # Enable strict mTLS mesh-wide to prevent plaintext internal traffic
    enableAutoMtls: true

    # Trust domain for identity verification
    trustDomain: cluster.local

    # DNS proxying for better resolution control
    dnsRefreshRate: 300s

---
# Enforce STRICT mTLS across the entire mesh
# This ensures all pod-to-pod communication is encrypted and mutually authenticated
# even when outbound traffic policy is permissive
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default-strict-mtls
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT

---
# Authorization policy to deny all traffic by default
# Teams must explicitly define AuthorizationPolicy resources to allow traffic
# This enforces least privilege at the mesh level
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: deny-all-default
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  # Empty spec with no rules denies all requests by default
  # Each namespace/service must define explicit allow policies
  {}

---
# Example: Restrict egress to known external APIs using AuthorizationPolicy
# Even with ALLOW_ANY outbound policy, you can layer authorization controls
# Uncomment and customize per namespace as needed
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-known-egress-hosts
  namespace: default
spec:
  action: ALLOW
  rules:
    # Allow internal mesh traffic (authenticated via mTLS)
    - from:
        - source:
            namespaces:
              - default
              - istio-system
    # Allow traffic to specific trusted external hosts
    - to:
        - operation:
            hosts:
              - "api.trusted-provider.com"
              - "*.googleapis.com"
              - "api.stripe.com"
            ports:
              - "443"

---
# Telemetry configuration for security observability
# Log all access for audit purposes without exposing sensitive headers
apiVersion: telemetry.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: Telemetry
metadata:
  name: mesh-default-logging
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  accessLogging:
    - providers:
        - name: envoy
      filter:
        expression: "response.code >= 400 || connection.mtls == false"

---
# NetworkPolicy to restrict which pods can reach the istio-proxy sidecar
# and prevent bypassing the mesh
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-non-mesh-egress
  namespace: default
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes:
    - Egress
  egress:
    # Allow DNS resolution
    - to: []
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
    # Allow traffic to istio control plane
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: istio-system
    # Allow HTTPS egress (routed through Envoy sidecar)
    - to: []
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 443
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 15001
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 15006
